subject: I vibe coded a video game. What went wrong surprised me.
body:
+ claim 50% off (or more) before AI April ends.

I vibe coded a video game the other day.

Here's what went wrong.

Денис,

Last weekend, I was sick and couldn't do much of anything. So I used Claude Code to build a simple game. Think Frogger meets Space Invaders (it was actually pretty fun... DM me if you want to check it out).

Of course, I expected the usual issues, like bugs and rough edges — but this wasn’t the biggest development hurdle.

The code was often correct. The problem was drift.

Before I explain how I tackled this challenge: here's your reminder that AI April is nearly over. There's only few days left to take advantage of our best discount of the year:

See your offer → (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VXgY222WFBnbW7kJJmM3SSnz0W4HyYrL5NqptCMZjDX43prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3pvV_QH7T7nmnwlN36Wt278tzSyVFptYV6Sdt7jN179vR5nh0y9W1bjC-p2QH1hsW3-gc4F73DnvkW8bT8KQ2l8qd9W47-kJy9d6hHQW2vY2w650YBkwVmvtPp7W3qf8W6PtmWp56dq5XW9b5X7P78pShXW6d4Z-84shn3BW6hBrl028Nd3XW9jfgy43LCRx2W2FyYZZ4yY9GNW7_BT2p8jhC_vW5dG0GB42F5yWW2vx9cM1NYk_jW2c0TkT1Gt1s1W2BTRpY1RkfMsW3p4vhS797NwqW4P1mJT2dntPZW4yxlfG7qg_tkW8-HqPD2BDYKSW4KMc4r7hQspVV6HT3b5h5ThhW1BHp6D2d265xf5fkTqx04 )

When the system loses the plot

AI doesn’t fail like traditional systems. It fails by quietly drifting out of alignment.

At one point, I gave Claude a clear plan for the game loop: state management, event handling, rendering order. It acknowledged everything and said it implemented the plan. When I checked, half of it wasn’t there.

Another time, I asked it to refactor logic across multiple files. It confidently replied: “Done.” In reality, it had wiped a section, didn’t stash anything, and had nothing to show for it. Four or five prompts, gone — and there's no refund on tokens.

What’s happening under the hood is simpler than it looks. Every time you send a prompt, the model rebuilds its understanding from the conversation so far. The model doesn’t really know what matters, it just works with what’s still in front of it.

You'll notice this gets worse as the conversation grows. Important details get buried or pushed out of the context window entirely. And sometimes it latches onto the wrong things — focusing on an earlier detail that’s no longer relevant while quietly ignoring the constraint you actually care about.

Once you go down the wrong path, you burn tokens reinforcing bad assumptions, stack fixes on shaky foundations, and end up doing more work than if you’d intervened early.

For personal projects, this is a manageable annoyance. But it's a real risk for production code.

Catching drift early

Good engineers don’t let AI wander. They tighten the loop early and reset direction before things compound.

And the best ones go a step further: they think about how to accomplish the most with the least amount of context burned. Fewer tokens wasted on bad turns means more runway for the work that actually matters.

This is why context management — knowing when to steer, when to reset, and how to stay precise — is the most underrated skill in AI-assisted development right now.

If you want to build that kind of discipline, our new vibe coding course (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VXgY222WFBnbW7kJJmM3SSnz0W4HyYrL5NqptCMZjDXn3prCCW95jsWP6lZ3lpW4mXkFF7ndFxDW8DW_HZ7sqQylW42WCBz2Sw_8MW5jMzrT1dJKJKW1dQWZt2qFvshM2Xx6yyrNqxW32pHBw69sx74W77vcRb5qgQQ9W37Zb401fBv4yW1w-GT75N2pSVN8BJlvpclx-LW2mCyQP6Bn7kHW2F-f9R5NCj3lW55RX2w829kS4W4Rs-bV4RmGGZVzjZsW3JqCFzN6qwMrckPctDW7jTqgw46HLgWW1-71Sk49r9HVW3w0Z6C5Y7-6mW25sGJn7T_WyWW78MFx63nJ3hsN2FNMZYztBVhW1qYZxg5hj-S_W1RnHzq1VFqqBW5x08vc7n0dSrN1W3ps4h1NpGW933_kx9dJ2K_W57xZkd7HtMK8W2c6QpH6rVRpCdKW4d804 )  is a good place to start. You'll learn how to guide AI effectively and get experience with in-demand tools like Lovable, Codex, and (of course) Claude Code.

It’s part of our AI April sale, and you just have a few days left to access 50% off our entire catalog (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VXgY222WFBnbW7kJJmM3SSnz0W4HyYrL5NqptCMZjDX43prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3pvV_QH7T7nmnwlN36Wt278tzSyVFptYV6Sdt7jN179vR5nh0y9W1bjC-p2QH1hsW3-gc4F73DnvkW8bT8KQ2l8qd9W47-kJy9d6hHQW2vY2w650YBkwVmvtPp7W3qf8W6PtmWp56dq5XW9b5X7P78pShXW6d4Z-84shn3BW6hBrl028Nd3XW9jfgy43LCRx2W2FyYZZ4yY9GNW7_BT2p8jhC_vW5dG0GB42F5yWW2vx9cM1NYk_jW2c0TkT1Gt1s1W2BTRpY1RkfMsW3p4vhS797NwqW4P1mJT2dntPZW4yxlfG7qg_tkW8-HqPD2BDYKSW4KMc4r7hQspVV6HT3b5h5ThhW1BHp6D2d265xf5fkTqx04 ) .

Where do you usually lose the thread — bad prompts or bad context?
